summary: Trump labelled the Revolutionary Guard a foreign terrorist organisation, a first against another state's military.
The Revolutionary Guard terrorist designation officially took effect alongside accompanying sanctions.
Washington dispatched a carrier strike group and bombers to the region.
Iran said it would expand uranium enrichment as new US sanctions hit Iranian metals.
The UAE reported four tankers had been sabotaged off the Fujairah coast.
Houthi drones launched from Yemen struck a major Saudi oil pipeline.
A rocket struck close to Baghdad's US embassy area, causing no casualties.
Trump said his administration was not pursuing regime change in Iran.
Japan's prime minister arrived in Tehran to attempt mediation between Washington and Iran.
Tankers were hit within Gulf of Oman waters as Japan's leader was visiting Tehran.
The Pentagon approved adding a thousand more US troops in the region.
Iranian troops downed a US military drone, with both sides disputing its airspace.
Trump said he aborted a retaliatory strike on Iran minutes before launch.
Iran executed a defence-ministry worker found guilty of CIA espionage.
Trump signed an executive order imposing financial sanctions on Khamenei and aides.
The US announced the deployment of F-22 stealth fighters to the region.
UN inspectors confirmed Iran had exceeded the deal's enriched uranium stockpile cap.
British forces in Gibraltar seized a tanker carrying Iranian crude bound for Syria.
Iran exceeded the agreed cap on uranium enrichment purity.
Gibraltar authorities detained the captain and his chief officer from the impounded Iranian vessel.
The Revolutionary Guard captured a British-flagged tanker passing through the Hormuz waterway.
Britain announced naval escorts for all UK-flagged ships transiting the strait.
Washington added Iran's foreign minister to its sanctions list.
A Gibraltar court order cleared the impounded Iranian tanker for departure.
Rouhani unveiled a domestically built air defence system at a Tehran ceremony.
Iran's foreign minister met Macron on the sidelines of the G7 summit in France.
A UN report confirmed Iran was further enriching uranium beyond agreement limits.
Washington sanctioned the Iranian space agency along with two research bodies.
Washington blacklisted an oil shipping network it linked to the Revolutionary Guard.
Iran started feeding gas into newer-generation centrifuges at its Natanz site.
Trump dismissed national security adviser Bolton, citing strong policy disagreements.
Drone strikes damaged two key Aramco oil sites in Saudi Arabia, slashing daily output.
At the UN General Assembly, Trump urged tighter economic measures against Iran.
Washington imposed sanctions on nine people in Khamenei's inner circle.
Iran resumed feeding uranium gas to centrifuges at the buried Fordow plant.
A naval grouping led by Washington began Gulf shipping patrols out of Bahrain.
Iran said its air defences had downed an unidentified foreign drone.
Nationwide unrest erupted in Iran following sudden fuel price increases.
The US sanctioned Iran's communications minister over internet censorship during the protests.
Revolutionary Guard chief Salami accused four foreign governments of fomenting Iranian unrest.
Iran said security agents had detained at least eight people accused of CIA links.
A US Navy ship intercepted what officials called Iranian missile components at sea.
Iran and the US exchanged prisoners through Swiss diplomatic mediation.
Rouhani unveiled a thirty-nine billion dollar budget framed as resisting US sanctions.
The US sanctioned Iran's largest airline and an associated shipping network.
Washington restricted visas for Iranian officials and sanctioned two Iranian judges.
An American contractor died when rockets hit an Iraqi base near Kirkuk.
US air strikes hit Kataib Hezbollah positions in Iraq and Syria, killing fighters.
Crowds tied to Iran-backed militias forced their way into Baghdad's US embassy grounds.
The embassy sit-in ended after security forces fired tear gas at protesters.
Esper warned that Iran or its allies might be planning further regional attacks.
A US drone strike at Baghdad airport killed Soleimani and militia commander Muhandis.
Trump said via Twitter that Washington had identified fifty-two Iranian retaliation targets.
Iraq's parliament voted to expel foreign troops; Iran abandoned remaining JCPOA enrichment limits.
Trump promised major retaliation if Iran responded; Washington denied Zarif a UN visa.
A stampede during the Soleimani funeral procession in Kerman killed at least fifty-six people.
Iran fired ballistic missiles at two Iraqi bases hosting US forces; no fatalities reported.
A coalition base in Kirkuk was hit by rockets, killing an American civilian contractor.
US warplanes struck Iran-backed Kataib Hezbollah positions in Iraq and Syria after the contractor's death.
Iran-aligned protesters breached the embassy grounds in Baghdad and set parts on fire.
The Pentagon confirmed Thursday night it had killed Soleimani to deter further Iranian attacks.
A US drone strike near Baghdad airport killed Soleimani and Iraqi paramilitary deputy commander al-Muhandis.
Trump said the action prevented a war; Pompeo told CNN it disrupted an impending attack.
Khamenei vowed harsh retaliation and named Qaani as Quds Force commander; Tehran saw mass protests.
Iran's UN envoy called the killing state terrorism and an opening of war.
A second airstrike near Taji and Green Zone rockets caused no reported injuries Saturday.
Trump tweeted that Washington had identified fifty-two Iranian retaliation targets, including cultural sites.
Iraqi crowds at the Soleimani funeral procession chanted Death to America.
The administration formally notified Congress of the strike under the War Powers Act.
Pompeo told CNN the precise timing of the imminent threat was not relevant.
On Limbaugh's radio show, Trump said previous presidents should have killed Soleimani years earlier.
Joint Chiefs chair Milley defended the intelligence as compelling though not specific about timing.
From the Oval Office, Trump described the strike partly as retaliation for past Iranian actions.
Pompeo and Esper offered new justifications, citing Soleimani's record and probable continuing operations against Americans.
Iran retaliated by firing roughly a dozen ballistic missiles toward Iraqi bases housing US forces.
At a Mar-a-Lago donor dinner, Trump described watching the strike unfold and mocked Soleimani.
